title: Parking Sensor System
subtitle: smart parking alert project!
overview: In this session, students will learn how a parking sensor system works using Arduino UNO and an ultrasonic sensor. The system detects nearby obstacles and alerts the user using a buzzer and LEDs.

images._note: Add actual parking sensor project photos, ultrasonic sensor setup, Arduino wiring, and student activity photos.

activity.title: Build a Smart Parking Sensor System
steps: Connect the ultrasonic sensor to Arduino
Connect Trigger pin to Arduino digital pin
Connect Echo pin to Arduino digital pin
Move an object near the sensor
Observe buzzer sound and LED changes based on distance

quiz.q: Which sensor is commonly used in a parking sensor project?
quiz.explanation: The HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor is used to measure distance in parking systems.

quiz.q: What does the ultrasonic sensor measure?
quiz.explanation: Ultrasonic sensors measure the distance between the sensor and an object.

quiz.q: What happens when a car gets too close to an obstacle?
quiz.explanation: The buzzer alerts the driver when the vehicle comes too close to an object.

quiz.q: What is the role of Arduino UNO in this project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO acts as the smart brain of the parking sensor system.

quiz.q: Which pin sends ultrasonic waves in HC-SR04?
quiz.explanation: The Trigger pin sends ultrasonic signals from the sensor.
The buzzer creates warning sounds during obstacle detection.

quiz.q: Why are LEDs used in parking sensor systems?
quiz.explanation: LEDs visually indicate safe or danger distance levels.

quiz.q: What type of waves are used in ultrasonic sensors?
quiz.explanation: Ultrasonic sensors use high-frequency sound waves.

quiz.q: Which component is used for temporary circuit building?
quiz.explanation: Breadboards are used to make temporary circuits without soldering.

quiz.q: Where are parking sensor systems commonly used?
quiz.explanation: Parking sensors are mainly used in cars to help drivers park safely.
